title: Standalone reference to 'this' is marked as a symbol-resolution error
body:
Thanks for taking the time to report this.

Can you attach an example that shows the issue?  (Must be openly licensed, ideally in test_regress format.)

I've modified t_class_uses_this.v to show the issue. Specifically, I've added a method that sets the 'addr' field by assigning 'this' to a temp variable, and assigning 'addr' via this temp variable (see set_indirect below):

```
class Cls;
   bit [3:0] addr;
   function void set(bit [3:0] addr);
   begin : body
     this.addr = addr;
   end : body
   endfunction

   function void set_indirect(bit [3:0] addr);
   begin : body
     Cls c = this;
     c.addr = addr;
   end : body
   endfunction

   extern function void setext(bit [3:0] addr);
endclass
```

When I do this, Verilator reports:
```
%Error: t/t_class_uses_this.v:16:14: Can't find definition of variable: 'this'
   16 |      Cls c = this;
      |              ^~~~
%Error: Exiting due to 1 error(s)
%Warning: vlt/t_class_uses_this: Exec of perl failed: %Error: t/t_class_uses_this.v:16:14: Can't find definition of variable: 'this'
```

What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?
I'm using the latest Git source.

What OS and distribution are you using?
CentOS 8

Would you be willing to try to fix Verilator yourself with assistance?
Yes, would be happy to. I've been able to recognize the 'this' reference in "visit(AstParseRef* nodep)". I tried to adapt the this-reference code from "visit(AstDot* nodep)", but this results in:
```
%Error: Internal Error: t/t_class_uses_this.v:16:14: ../V3Width.cpp:4812: Visit function missing? Widthed expectation for this node: PARSEREF 0x2340f10 <e343#> {d16ao}  this [$root]
```

I suspect that I need to make a nodep->replaceWith() call, but I'm uncertain what to pass to provide the representation of the class instance.
